source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Consider ACK TEE-based when running confidential computing

input_row.source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
input_row.title: Consider ACK TEE-based when running confidential computing
input_row.description: ACK	provides	TEE-based	confidential	computing.	This	is	a	cloud-native	and	all-in-one solution	based	on	Intel	Software	Guard	Extensions	(SGX).	It	aims	to	deliver	and	manage trusted	applications	and	confidential	computing	applications	by	ensuring	the	security, integrity,	and	confidentiality	of	data	in	use.
input_row.rationale: ACK	TEE-based	confidential	computing	allows	you	to	isolate	sensitive	data	and	code	in	a trusted	execution	environment.	This	prevents	the	rest	part	of	the	system	from	accessing the	data.	Encrypted	data	in	a	trusted	execution	environment	is	unavailable	to	other applications,	the	BIOS,	the	operating	system,	the	kernel,	administrators,	O&M	personnel, cloud	vendors,	and	hardware	components	except	CPUs.	This	reduces	the	possibility	of sensitive	data	leaks	and	simplifies	data	management.
input_row.audit: Remediation: For	create	cluster: 1. Log	on	to	the	ACK	console	https://cs.console.aliyun.com/. 2. In	the	left-side	navigation	pane,	click	Clusters. 3. On	the	Clusters	page,	click	Create	Kubernetes	Cluster	in	the	upper-right	corner	of the page. 4. In	the	Select	Cluster	Template	dialog	box,	find	Confidential	Computing	Cluster	in	the Managed	Clusters	section	and	click	Create. 5. On	the	ACK	managed	edition	tab,	configure	the	cluster	and	set	the	parameters	as required in	the	following	list: • Zone:	Only	ECS	Bare	Metal	instances	of	the	ecs.ebmhfg5.2xlarge	type	support confidential	computing	clusters.	Make	sure	that	this	instance	type	is	available	in	the selected	zone. • Container	Runtime:	Docker	18.09.2. 277	|	P a g e • Instance	Type:	Select	ecs.ebmhfg5.2xlarge	of	the	ECS	Bare	Metal	instance	family. • Operating	System:	AliyunLinux	2.xxxx. • Network	Plug-in:	Flannel. • Custom	Image:	Do	not	use	custom	images.	Otherwise,	confidential	computing	may be	unavailable. Default	Value: By	default,	ACK	TEE-based	confidential	computing	is	disabled References: 1. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/164536.htm 2. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/150518.htm 3. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/164537.htm CIS	Controls: Version	7 18.9	Separate	Production	and	Non-Production	Systems Maintain	separate	environments	for	production	and	nonproduction	systems.	Developers should	not	have	unmonitored	access	to	production	environments. 278	|	P a g e
input_row.references: 1. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/164536.htm 2. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/150518.htm 3. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/164537.htm CIS	Controls: Version	7 18.9	Separate	Production	and	Non-Production	Systems Maintain	separate	environments	for	production	and	nonproduction	systems.	Developers should	not	have	unmonitored	access	to	production	environments. 278	|	P a g e

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**

- This control involves the configuration and creation of a Kubernetes cluster using the Alibaba Cloud Container Service for Kubernetes (ACK) with confidential computing settings, specifically TEE-based architecture. The audit and remediation sections provide instructions on how to create such clusters but do not mention any Alibaba Cloud API or CLI commands to verify an existing cluster's compliance with the requirement for TEE-based confidential computing.

- The audit description is more procedural about setting up clusters rather than verifying an existing configuration. This likely involves visual inspection and confirmation of cluster creation parameters, thus requiring human judgment to ensure that clusters are correctly configured.

- The references provided are helpful for understanding the setup process but do not include a direct method for API-based verification of the settings related to TEE-based confidential computing.

- Given the nature of the task, involving the inspection of cluster creation and the types of instances used, it is more suited to a manual verification approach.

**MANUAL STEPS:**

1. Log in to the Alibaba Cloud Management Console.
2. Navigate to the Container Service for Kubernetes (ACK) console.
3. In the left-side navigation pane, click "Clusters."
4. Review the list of existing Kubernetes clusters to identify any that might use confidential computing.
5. For each relevant cluster, check the instance type used. Verify that it is an `ecs.ebmhfg5.2xlarge` type, which supports TEE-based confidential computing.
6. Confirm that the cluster configuration meets other specified parameters, such as using AliyunLinux as the operating system, Docker 18.09.2 as the container runtime, and Flannel as the network plug-in.
7. Ensure that no custom images are used if they might disrupt the intended confidential computing configuration.
8. Document each verification step to confirm compliance with the CIS benchmark.

**SOURCE:** CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
